name: find-up
usage_summary: Used in a single location: cli-kit/src/public/node/fs.ts imports findUp as internalFindUp from 'find-up'. It is exposed through the public findPathUp() function, which wraps it with path normalization. findPathUp() is called across the codebase wherever a config file or package.json needs to be located by walking up the directory tree.
how_to_remove: Node.js has no built-in find-up equivalent, but the functionality is a simple recursive directory walk (check for file existence, move to parent, stop at root). A self-contained ~20-line async helper can replace find-up entirely within fs.ts, behind the existing findPathUp() wrapper. The public API stays unchanged. Alternatively, the locate-path or escalade packages are lighter options. Low risk since the call is already isolated behind a single wrapper function.
replacement_reason: The dependency is used only through a single wrapper function. Inlining a small recursive path-walker (~20 lines using fs/promises.access and path.dirname) eliminates the dependency with negligible effort and no API changes to callers.
